+ DERIVATIVES LAWYER COLIN RICE JOINS JONES DAY IN SINGAPORE

Colin Rice has joined Jones Day as a partner in its Financial Markets Practice. He is based in the Firm's Singapore Office.

Mr. Rice is an experienced derivatives lawyer who has counseled clients on a broad range of financial markets transactions, with a particular focus on OTC derivatives, structured products, structured finance transactions, repos, and securities lending. He works with global investment banks, retail banks, funds, corporates, and service providers. He also advises on uncleared derivatives margin reform and collateral arrangements, default management, and the impact of global regulatory developments on derivatives businesses and documentation.

"Colin is an important addition to our global derivatives team. Asia is a fast-growing market with more and more netting friendly jurisdictions, and he is among the most experienced lawyers in the region on these matters," said Alban Caillemer du Ferrage, chair of Jones Day's Financial Markets department for EMEA and APAC. "Colin served as a director in the Singapore office of one of the world's largest investment banks. We will be able to work with our largest clients on their most complex derivatives transactions there. I am delighted to welcome him to our global group."

+ STEPTOE LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ STEPTOE & JOHNSON LLP CONOR TUCKER APPOINTED CHAIR OF THE BEVERLY HILLS BAR ASSOCIATION'S APPELLATE LAW SECTION

(January 6, 2026, Los Angeles) - Steptoe LLP associate Conor Tucker has been appointed as Chair of the Beverly Hills Bar Association's Appellate Law Section for the 2025 - 2026 bar year.

Conor is an experienced litigator who has represented clients at every level of the federal court system - including serving as Counsel of Record before the United States Supreme Court. He has guided cases from the earliest stages of investigation through trial and appeal and has argued key motions and appeals in both state and federal courts. Conor's advocacy - in briefing and at argument- has led to published, precedent-setting decisions, including issues of first impression. In the California Court of Appeal alone, this includes litigating issues of first impression regarding the impact of COIVD-19 on force majeure clauses in live music performance contracts and state regulation of video streaming services. In the United States Supreme Court, he routinely serves as Counsel of Record for Members of Congress as amici.

He has handled a wide variety of matters, from contract disputes to intellectual property cases involving trademarks, trade secrets, copywrights, and patents, as well as constitutional, criminal procedure, and due process issues. His clients span a broad range of industries, including technology, energy, real estate, retail, apparel, mining, entertainment, banking, healthcare, social media, and computer sciences.

+ STEPTOE NAMED TO LEXOLOGY 100: DATA 2026

(December 22, 2025, Washington, DC) - Steptoe has been recognized in the 2026 edition of Lexology 100: Data, Lexology's annual guide profiling the world's leading data law firms. The Lexology 100: Data, formerly known as the GDR 100, is the only global ranking that evaluates firms' capabilities, track records, and market reputations across the full spectrum of data law, including privacy and data protection, AI regulation, online content moderation, cybersecurity, B2B data disputes, and the use of IP and confidentiality frameworks to safeguard proprietary information.

Lexology notes that the 150 firms recognized this year "fully merit the accolade," following a rigorous assessment process focused on firms' depth of practice, major matters, and sustained representation of companies with complex global data needs. The ranking reflects not only personal data work, but also the rapidly growing field of non‑personal data regulation, including AI‑related mandates, online harms laws, and cybersecurity frameworks such as DORA and NIS2.

+ STEPTOE & JOHNSON LLP THE HILL NAMES LESLIE BELCHER, ELIZABETH BURKS, AND ROWAN BOST TO TOP LOBBYISTS 2025

(December 12, 2025, Washington, DC) - Leslie Belcher, Elizabeth Burks, and Rowan Bost have been named to The Hill's Top Lobbyists 2025 list. Selected from more than 1,800 nominations the lobbyists on this list represent the industry's savviest, most influential and well-connected advocates across Capitol Hill. The honorees span veterans and up-and-comers, retired military officials and academics, former lawmakers and staffers, and are intended to reflect the wide ranges of experiences and backgrounds of the city's most influential K Street forces.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADDS ALEXANDRA CAMPBELL-DIEHL AS LABOR & EMPLOYMENT ASSOCIATE IN BOSTON

BOSTON - April 22, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has added Alexandra Campbell-Diehl to its Boston office as an associate in the firm's Labor & Employment Practice.

Campbell-Diehl advises employers on matters including single-plaintiff and class action employment litigation related to wrongful termination, harassment, discrimination, retaliation, and more, as well as workplace investigations and corporate transactions. Her work focuses on traditional labor law issues, including employer rights and obligations related to unions, and advising on union organizing, collective bargaining, and labor law developments. She has defended employers in attorney general investigations, state and federal litigation, and proceedings before the National Labor Relations Board, Department of Labor, Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, and the Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination.

Campbell-Diehl earned her J.D. from Boston College Law School and a B.S. from Cornell University.

"We are pleased to welcome Alex to the Boston Labor & Employment team," Terence P. McCourt and David J. Dykeman, co-managing shareholders of Greenberg Traurig's Boston office, said in a joint statement. "She brings a strong foundation in both employment law and traditional labor law, with experience advising employers on various employment matters in the workplace. Alex's addition enhances our capacity to support clients facing an increasingly dynamic and regulated employment landscape."

+ MORGAN LEWIS ADVISES QUINTILLION ON SALE TO GCI HOLDINGS LLC

PITTSBURGH, April 22, 2026: Morgan Lewis is representing Q Gateway Intermediate Holdings LLC (Quintillion) in connection with a definitive agreement under which GCI Holdings LLC (GCI), a wholly owned subsidiary of GCI Liberty Inc. and Grain Management LLC, will acquire 100% of Quintillion's equity. The transaction will combine Quintillion's more than 1,800 miles of existing subsea and terrestrial fiber and approximately 1,500 miles of planned expansion with GCI's statewide network and operations, further supporting the delivery of reliable, high-speed connectivity across Alaska.

Quintillion is a communications infrastructure provider in Alaska, providing subsea and terrestrial fiber connectivity primarily on a wholesale basis through a total network spanning more than 3,300 route miles of subsea and terrestrial fiber. Headquartered in Alaska, GCI provides data, mobile, voice, and managed services to consumer, business, government, and carrier customers throughout the state, serving more than 200 communities.

Morgan Lewis partners Kim Taylor, Greer Longer, Harry Robins, Paul Gordon, and Ulises Pin and associates Matt Cubin, Frank Ren, and Andreas Andrews are advising Quintillion.

Source: Company Website

+ MORGAN LEWIS ANNOUNCES MICHAEL RINEHART AS CHIEF INFORMATION OFFICER

PHILADELPHIA, April 22, 2026: Morgan Lewis is pleased to announce that Michael Rinehart has joined the firm as chief information officer, strengthening the firm's technology platform and cybersecurity capabilities. Michael joins from Dechert, where he served as CIO, overseeing the growth and implementation of the firm's information technology strategy.

In his role, Michael will lead the firm's global information technology function, focusing on the continued development of secure, reliable, and scalable systems that support the firm's lawyers and the delivery of client service. He brings significant experience in cybersecurity and enterprise infrastructure, further reinforcing the firm's approach to information security and risk management. Michael will work in close coordination with Chief AI & Knowledge Management Officer Colleen Nihill and her team to ensure alignment between the firm's technology platform and its data, AI, and knowledge initiatives.

Michael brings more than 25 years of experience across professional services and industry and focuses on information technology strategy, infrastructure, and operations, including enterprise systems, cybersecurity, and digital transformation. Prior to Dechert, Michael served as chief information officer at Fox Rothschild, where he led technology strategy and operations for a decade. Earlier in his career, he held a range of technology leadership roles in professional services and industry.

"Technology is central to how we operate and deliver value to our clients," said Tony Licata, chief operating officer of Morgan Lewis. "Michael brings a strong track record of leading enterprise technology functions in complex professional services organizations, with particular depth in infrastructure and cybersecurity. His collaborative approach will help ensure our lawyers have the systems and support they need, while we continue to advance our technology priorities in coordination with our AI and knowledge management initiatives."

Source: Company Website

+ MORGAN LEWIS REPRESENTS LIME ROCK NEW ENERGY ON CLOSING $640 MILLION FUND II

NEW YORK, April 22, 2026: Morgan Lewis advised Lime Rock New Energy in the closing of Lime Rock New Energy Fund II (Fund II) with total commitments of $640 million. The fund closed well above its $500 million target and just below its $650 million hard cap, representing a doubling of committed capital relative to its predecessor fund.

Lime Rock New Energy is a growth-focused private equity partner for entrepreneurs and management teams building differentiated, innovative businesses across the energy transition. Fund II attracted commitments from a range of institutional investors, including asset managers, university endowments, and climate-focused family offices, with approximately one-third of commitments coming from European and Asian institutions.

The Morgan Lewis team that advised Lime Rock New Energy included partners Daniel A. Losk, Richard S. Zarin, Steven Lightstone, and Christine Ayako Schleppegrell and associates Joe Koronkowski, Thomas J. Crociata, and Yara Ismael.

urn:contentItem:6JGB-S5D3-RTDH-2471-00000-00FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP - THE RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS AND TRENDS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:00:48ZLexisNexisSTOCKFOX ROTHSCHILD LLP - THE RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS AND TRENDS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP

PermID4297411712Websitehttp://www.foxrothschild.com/IndustrySpecialized Maritime-shipping law Firm

ACTIVITIES:

Fox, Rothschild, O'Brien & Frankel, LLP provides legal services to businesses and individual clients throughout the greater Delaware Valley and central and southern New Jersey, as well as across the United States. The firms area of practice includes business, corporate and securities, labor and employment, real estate, creditors' rights, litigation, and tax and estates law. The firm was founded in 1907 and is based Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. It has offices in Doylestown, Exton, and Lansdale, Pennsylvania; Atlantic City, and Princeton, New Jersey; and Wilmington, Delaware.

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP [6 Press Releases]

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP CATHERINE MITCHELL AND THOMAS TERRELL SELECTED TO NORTH CAROLINA LAWYERS WEEKLY'S REAL ESTATE POWER LIST

Fox Rothschild congratulates Catherine B. Mitchell and Thomas E. Terrell Jr. who were named to North Carolina Lawyers Weekly's 2025 Real Estate Power List, which highlights North Carolina's leading real estate attorneys.

Mitchell, selected to the list for the second consecutive year, has handled more than $2 billion in transactions throughout her 14-year career. Mitchell is noted by North Carolina Lawyers Weekly as a "skilled advocate for national and regional clients in commercial real estate transactions throughout the development process." She advises clients on property tax exemptions for low-income housing and handles all aspects of commercial real estate transactions, including acquisitions, dispositions, leasing and financing. Her clients range from landlords and tenants to health care providers, developers and life insurance companies.

Mitchell is a Fellow of the American College of Mortgage Attorneys and a member of the University of North Carolina Asheville Board of Trustees. She also provides pro bono representation to businesses in Asheville that were affected by flooding from Hurricane Helene.

With four decades of practice, Terrell has handled zoning and land use matters in 88 of North Carolina's 100 counties, and in over 450 local governments across six states. He is hired in "hard-to-permit" industries, including mining, landfills and renewable energy.

Source: Company Website

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP HEIDY VAQUERANO NAMED TO LA BUSINESS JOURNAL'S LEADERS OF INFLUENCE LIST

Fox Rothschild congratulates Heidy Vaquerano for being named to Los Angeles Business Journal's Leaders of Influence: Thriving in Their 40s list.

Vaquerano is among 40 individuals recognized for their skills and ability to "help set growth in motion for businesses in virtually any industry."

A partner in the firm's Entertainment & Sports Law Department, Vaquerano represents musicians, producers, actors, writers, production companies, record labels, tech startups, private-equity funds and other entertainment industry players.

She possesses a deep understanding of the evolving laws and technology impacting the music industry. Vaquerano works with artists and rights companies at the forefront of a major shift in the industry to help capitalize on market opportunities.

Notably, she has represented intellectual property-focused investment firm HarbourView Equity Partners since its founding in 2021. "In the past calendar year, she was instrumental in the client securing a combined $3 billion in debt financing through securitization for catalogues," the Los Angeles Business Journal reported. The debt financings were supported by more than 75 music catalogs from a variety of artists whose acquisitions were negotiated by Vaquerano and the Fox team.

Source: Company Website

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD DONATES $1 MILLION TO SUPPORT YOUTH PROGRAMS THROUGH GENERATION FOX INITIATIVE

Fox Rothschild's Generation Fox community service initiative has donated $1 million, more than 5,000 items and 800+ service hours to more than 225 organizations that empower youth from coast to coast.

"Giving back has always been deeply personal to us and our firm culture - it's part of who we are," said Firmwide Managing Partner Todd A. Rodriguez. "With Generation Fox, we've put youth at the heart of our charitable efforts and community engagement. Our goal is to make a difference, and the results speak for themselves."

Launched in July 2024, Generation Fox invests in and supports youth in the communities where the firm has offices across the U.S. The initiative encompasses a multitude of ways that Fox gives back to its communities, with a focus on children up to age 18.

During the holidays, Generation Fox is stepping up its commitment to supporting local schools by providing essential supplies and financial assistance. Our offices have organized events or donation drives addressing the most urgent needs of schools in their communities.

"While every one of our offices has its own unique character, we are united by a shared purpose: to empower youth in our communities," said Holly Lentz Kleeman, Chief Business Development and Marketing Officer at Fox. "By collaborating with our local schools we are making a shared investment in the future."

Generation Fox was designed to be flexible so that lawyers, practice groups, offices and the firm's business professionals can each work and donate in ways that make sense in their cities and neighborhoods. The goals are equally expansive: to support groups that empower kids in their education and in their play, in cultural development, in nutrition, in health and well-being, and through protection of their legal rights.

Source: Company Website

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP CAROLYN RICHMOND HONORED BY NEW YORK BUSINESS JOURNAL

Fox Rothschild congratulates Carolyn Richmond, who was recognized by the New York Business Journal as one of ten honorees who "exemplify influence in both their professional and their personal endeavors."

Part of the publication's Women of Influence program, Richmond's selection reflects her significant and lasting impact on the hospitality sector and her commitment to advancing workplace compliance and innovation.

Chair of the firm's Hospitality Practice, Richmond is a trusted employment attorney to New York's restaurant industry. She helps clients address evolving challenges, including wage and hour laws, workplace regulations, union organizing and crisis management.

Source: Company Website

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP RICK COUGHLIN, JEFF MACHARG AND BRAD RISINGER SELECTED AS MANAGING PARTNERS TO WATCH

Fox Rothschild congratulates Greensboro, Charlotte and Raleigh Office Managing Partners, Richard A. Coughlin, Jeffrey P. MacHarg and Bradley M. Risinger, who were all recognized by North Carolina Lawyers Weekly as Managing Partners to Watch.

The annual feature spotlights managing partners who are "equal parts administrative genius and skilled legal practitioner."

Coughlin, selected for the second consecutive year, became Greensboro Managing Partner in 2021. Under his leadership the office has grown to become firm's largest in the Carolinas. Coughlin also serves as the firm's Deputy General Counsel where he plays an important part in helping lawyers navigate difficult situations. He told North Carolina Lawyers Weekly that, "if you put in enough effort and promote good communication, you usually can find a solution that benefits everyone." With an eye toward giving back to the community, Coughlin worked with the office to pledge $100,000 to support a Cone Health mobile health unit that will provide essential medical services to underserved communities in North Carolina.

Honoring his commitment to leadership and adaptability, this marks MacHarg's third year on the list. Since assuming the Charlotte Managing Partner role in 2021, he has doubled the number of attorneys in the office. MacHarg also led the team's relocation effort, moving the office into the heart of Charlotte's central business district. He maintains an active litigation practice with clients spanning a variety of industries. Colleagues continue to be inspired by the passion he brings to both roles, noting to North Carolina Lawyers Weekly that MacHarg remains "fully engaged and has made a significant impact on office life."

Risinger took the reins as Raleigh Managing Partner in 2022. He brings 35 years of legal experience to the role. A seasoned trial lawyer whose practice centers on commercial disputes, he is highlighted for representing law firms and media outlets denied access to public records by law enforcement - all cases that raise concerns about First Amendment rights and government transparency. A zealous advocate, Risinger says that "especially in today's climate, this is a fight that's always worth waging." North Carolina Lawyers Weekly also cited his involvement in local community organizations, including United Way of North Carolina and NC 211, the state's health and human services referral network.

Source: Company Website

Fox Rothschild welcomes Kieron A. Frazier to the Chicago office as a Partner in the Gaming Group.

"With Illinois' gaming industry continuing to boom, we are thrilled to welcome Kieron to our Chicago office," said Gordon E. Gouveia, Fox's Chicago Office Managing Partner. "Kieron's depth of experience in the gaming sector, combined with his unique background in sports law make him a valuable addition to our respected gaming industry team."

A corporate and transactional attorney, Frazier focuses his practice on the gaming industry.

He provides advice on regulatory compliance, licensure and internal investigations in addition to transactions and other general corporate matters. Frazier's clients include leaders in sports wagering, fantasy sports, iGaming, horse tracks, video gaming and lotteries. He also supports tribal licensees and social gaming operators and investors.

In addition, Frazier counsels clients on a variety of corporate and finance matters, including team-level transactional work, financing, media transactions, mergers and acquisitions.

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

+ STEPTOE & JOHNSON LLP CONOR TUCKER APPOINTED CHAIR OF THE BEVERLY HILLS BAR ASSOCIATION'S APPELLATE LAW SECTION

(January 6, 2026, Los Angeles) - Steptoe LLP associate Conor Tucker has been appointed as Chair of the Beverly Hills Bar Association's Appellate Law Section for the 2025 - 2026 bar year.

Conor is an experienced litigator who has represented clients at every level of the federal court system - including serving as Counsel of Record before the United States Supreme Court. He has guided cases from the earliest stages of investigation through trial and appeal and has argued key motions and appeals in both state and federal courts. Conor's advocacy - in briefing and at argument- has led to published, precedent-setting decisions, including issues of first impression. In the California Court of Appeal alone, this includes litigating issues of first impression regarding the impact of COIVD-19 on force majeure clauses in live music performance contracts and state regulation of video streaming services. In the United States Supreme Court, he routinely serves as Counsel of Record for Members of Congress as amici.

He has handled a wide variety of matters, from contract disputes to intellectual property cases involving trademarks, trade secrets, copywrights, and patents, as well as constitutional, criminal procedure, and due process issues. His clients span a broad range of industries, including technology, energy, real estate, retail, apparel, mining, entertainment, banking, healthcare, social media, and computer sciences.

BOSTON - April 22, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has added Alexandra Campbell-Diehl to its Boston office as an associate in the firm's Labor & Employment Practice.

PITTSBURGH, April 22, 2026: Morgan Lewis is representing Q Gateway Intermediate Holdings LLC (Quintillion) in connection with a definitive agreement under which GCI Holdings LLC (GCI), a wholly owned subsidiary of GCI Liberty Inc. and Grain Management LLC, will acquire 100% of Quintillion's equity. + MORGAN LEWIS ANNOUNCES MICHAEL RINEHART AS CHIEF INFORMATION OFFICER

PHILADELPHIA, April 22, 2026: Morgan Lewis is pleased to announce that Michael Rinehart has joined the firm as chief information officer, strengthening the firm's technology platform and cybersecurity capabilities. Michael joins from Dechert, where he served as CIO, overseeing the growth and implementation of the firm's information technology strategy.

In his role, Michael will lead the firm's global information technology function, focusing on the continued development of secure, reliable, and scalable systems that support the firm's lawyers and the delivery of client service. + MORGAN LEWIS REPRESENTS LIME ROCK NEW ENERGY ON CLOSING $640 MILLION FUND II

NEW YORK, April 22, 2026: Morgan Lewis advised Lime Rock New Energy in the closing of Lime Rock New Energy Fund II (Fund II) with total commitments of $640 million. value.Title: Weekly renewables M&A round-up (May 4 - 8)

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHW-6WN3-RSDG-X4K7-00000-00Weekly renewables M&A round-up (May 4 - 8)2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:00:47ZLexisNexisWeekly renewables M&A round-up (May 4 - 8)SeeNews

Renewables Now covered M&A stories about Scatec, Octopus, JinkoSolar and others in the past week. Below is the full list:

-- German battery maker CMBlu raises EUR 50m, reaches unicorn status

German battery maker CMBlu Energy has reached a valuation of over EUR 1 billion in a series C round, securing financing to back the deployment of a non-lithium, long-duration energy storage technology.

-- IFM's Mobius acquires Air Liquide's biogas assets in US, Europe

Houston, Texas-headquartered renewable natural gas (RNG) firm Mobius Renewables has finalised the acquisition of biogas production assets from Air Liquide across the US, France, Norway and Sweden.

-- Repsol selling 49% of 706-MW of Spanish wind, solar to Masdar - report

Spanish multi-energy group Repsol SA has agreed to sell a 49% stake in a 706-MW wind and solar farm portfolio in Spain to Abu Dhabi-based renewables company Masdar, Spanish business daily Cinco Dias reported, citing unnamed sources close to the deal.

-- Brussels clears EDF, Eurus JV for 120-MW BESS project in Poland

A special-purpose vehicle established specifically for the development, construction, and operation of a 120-MW battery energy storage system (BESS) project located in Kobiernice, Poland, will be run as a joint venture (JV) between EDF Renewables Polska and Eurus Energy Europe BV, after the European Commission said it had approved the deal for joint control of the entity.

-- German solar manufacturer Soluxtec files for insolvency

German solar module manufacturer Soluxtec GmbH filed an application for insolvency proceedings with the Bitburg district court.

-- Aspen Power acquires 6.44-MW New Jersey rooftop solar portfolio

US distributed generation firm Aspen Power said it has acquired two operating rooftop solar systems totalling 6.44 MWdc in New Jersey from real estate developer The Avidan Group.

-- Second-life battery firm Moment Energy raises over USD 40m in Series B

Canada-based EV battery repurposing firm Moment Energy said it had raised more than USD 40 million in a Series B funding round to expand manufacturing and deploy second-life energy storage systems across North America.

-- Fervo buying Baywa re's solar EPC unit in Italy

Italian technical services and energy management firm Fervo said it has acquired BayWa r.e. Power Solutions Srl, the Italy-based solar installation company of German renewables developer BayWa r.e. AG.

-- E energija sheds Lithuanian renewable project duo to Energix

Lithuanian renewables developer E energija group has offloaded two shovel-ready renewable energy projects in Lithuania to a unit of Israeli power producer Energix Renewable Energies Ltd.

-- National Bank of Egypt buys into Scatec's Obelisk project

National Bank of Egypt (NBE), the largest commercial bank in Egypt, has acquired a 20% economic interest in the gigawatt-scale Obelisk solar-plus-storage project in Nagaa Hammadi.

-- KGAL sells 75-MW Polish onshore wind portfolio to Octopus

Independent investment and asset manager KGAL GmbH & Co KG said it has sold a 75-MW portfolio of onshore wind parks in Poland to Octopus Energy Generation, a unit of Britain's Octopus Energy Group.

-- Octopus to buy European wind portfolio in EUR-584m deal

Octopus Energy Generation, a unit of Britain's Octopus Energy Group, has spent EUR 584 million on buying a 321-MW portfolio of onshore wind assets across France, Germany and Poland as part of its European expansion.

-- Morrow Batteries pushed into bankruptcy as financing efforts fail

Norwegian industrial battery technology company Morrow Batteries ASA has decided to file for bankruptcy proceedings after failing to secure fresh funding and a new industrial investor in time.

-- Vopak to buy majority stake in Dutch battery storage developer GES

Dutch tank storage company Royal Vopak said it has agreed in principle to acquire a majority stake in compatriot battery storage developer Green Energy Storage (GES), subject to a final investment decision (FID).

-- Encavis grows German wind fleet with 80-MW purchase

German independent power producer Encavis AG has acquired a roughly 80-MW onshore wind farm in the federal state of Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania, further expanding its wind fleet at home.

-- Verdian snaps up 117-MWp agrivoltaic portfolio in Italy

Dutch-based independent power producer Verdian, a portfolio company of Nuveen Infrastructure, unveiled the acquisition of a portfolio of three agrivoltaic projects in Sicily with a combined capacity of 117 MWp.

-- Elements Green acquires 300-MW Newarthill BESS project in Scotland

London-based renewables developer Elements Green has acquired the 300-MW Newarthill battery energy storage system (BESS) project in Scotland from developer Geocore, expanding its UK energy storage pipeline.

-- Ørsted buys 150-MW Salzburg BESS project in Michigan

Danish energy company Ørsted A/S has acquired the 150-MW Salzburg battery energy storage system (BESS) project in the US state ofMichigan from developer ESA Solar Energy LLC, with market intelligence firm Ascend Analytics acting as exclusive adviser on the transaction.

-- Belgian co-ops join 100-MW battery project in Ruien

A Belgian consortium comprising green power producer Aspiravi and energy cooperatives Ecopower and BeauVent, alongside Nippon Koei Energy Europe (NKEE), has started building a 100-MW/250-MWh battery energy storage system (BESS) complex in Ruien, western Belgium.

-- Nuvve's Japan unit raises funds in JKISS transaction for expansion, IPO

Nuvve Japan K.K. (NVJ), a unit of US-based energy storage aggregator Nuvve Holding Corp, said it had raised JPY 200 million through a JKISS-style convertible equity financing round to support business expansion in Japan.

-- Edisun Power plans CHF 440m deal with Smartenergy

Swiss solar power producer Edisun Power Europe AG plans a merger with its strategic partner, private investment firm Smartenergy Group AG, with the deal requiring a capital increase of up to CHF 440 million.

-- Sosteneo to buy Italian biomethane project portfolio

Asset manager Sosteneo SGR SpA has agreed to buy an Italian agricultural biomethane portfolio, which includes four fully permitted projects in Lombardy.

-- Acciona Energia divesting 361-MW wind farm portfolio in Spain - report

Spanish renewables group Acciona Energia has hired BNP Paribas to advise on the sale of a wind farm portfolio of about 361 MW in Spain, business newspaper Expansion reported, citing unnamed market sources.

-- Microporous gets funds for 65-GWh Virginia battery separators factory

US battery separator maker Microporous, which is a portfolio company of Trent Capital Partners, has received final financing for a new manufacturing facility in Danville, Virginia, and plans to begin construction as soon as May.

-- Mubadala invests in US renewable software firm Power Factors

Abu Dhabi's state-owned holding company Mubadala Investment Company has bought a minority stake in Power Factors LLC, a US provider of renewable energy operations and asset performance management software.

-- JUWI sells 25-MW wind project in Germany to Diehl Group unit

German renewables developer JUWI GmbH said it has sold the 25-MW Gundersweiler 2 wind farm project in southwestern Germany to industrial group Diehl.

-- Wave energy firm Panthalassa nets USD 140m from Peter Thiel, others

US renewable energy and ocean technology company Panthalassa has closed a USD-140-million funding round, led by entrepreneur and venture capitalist Peter Thiel, to scale its technology for wave-powered offshore AI computing systems with a pilot manufacturing site, among other initiatives.

-- JinkoSolar to cede control of GW-scale US manufacturing ops to PE firm

Private equity firm FH Capital has inked a deal to acquire, for an undisclosed amount, a 75.1% majority stake in Chinese solar manufacturer JinkoSolar's US manufacturing subsidiary, committing to expanding domestic solar and battery storage production.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJK-BRM3-SCMM-W13D-00000-00Newark Liberty Expands Solar Infrastructure with 5-Megawatt Project2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:00:47ZLexisNexisNEWSNewark Liberty Expands Solar Infrastructure with 5-Megawatt Project

Port Authority initiative advances airport sustainability goals while increasing renewable energy generation across EWR campus. The Port Authority of New York and New Jersey plans to expand...

Port Authority initiative advances airport sustainability goals while increasing renewable energy generation across EWR campus. The Port Authority ofNew York and New Jersey plans to expand solar energy infrastructure at Newark Liberty International Airpor...

Port Authority initiative advances airport sustainability goals while increasing renewable energy generation across EWR campus. The Port Authority ofNew York and New Jersey plans to expand solar energy infrastructure at Newark Liberty International Airport (EWR) through a new 5-megawatt solar project spanning five airport sites. The project is expected to generate nearly 5.9 million kilowatt-hours of electricity annually, enough to power more than 550 homes, while supporting the agency’s long-term emissions reduction and net-zero goals. Port Authority officials said the installation will include rooftop, parking lot and parking garage solar systems located across the airport campus, including Terminal C parking facilities and rideshare areas. “Newark Liberty has been central to our sustainability story for years,” said Port Authority Chairman Kevin O’Toole. “Today we’re adding to that record with 5 megawatts of new solar capacity across five more sites.” The project will be developed through a power purchase agreement with SunLight General Capital, which will design, build, finance, own and maintain the installations while the Port Authority purchases the generated electricity at a fixed rate. Construction is expected to begin this year, with completion targeted for 2028. The Port Authority said the expansion will increase agency-wide solar generation capacity to nearly 20 megawatts across approximately 32,000 solar panels, representing nearly a tenfold increase in solar capacity since 2021. Officials also said the project supports broader agency sustainability initiatives, including a commitment to reduce direct emissions by 50 percent by 2030 and achieve net-zero emissions by 2050. The solar expansion is expected to avoid nearly 2,000 metric tons of CO2 emissions annually, equivalent to removing approximately 460 gasoline-powered vehicles from the road each year.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHV-KBF3-RRNH-J4VV-00000-00Bad for Business2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:59:21ZLexisNexisBLOGBad for BusinessScott Lemieux

It is at this point banal to note that Mark Burnett making a mass audience believe that Donald Trump was a business genius is a critical factor in explaining his political raise — a nontrivial number of his marginal voters think that he will personally make them rich. But this also plays into a larger...

It is at this point banal to note that Mark Burnett making a mass audience believe that Donald Trump was a business genius is a critical factor in explaining his political raise — a nontrivial number of his marginal voters think that he will personally mak...

It is at this point banal to note that Mark Burnett making a mass audience believe that Donald Trump was a business genius is a critical factor in explaining his political raise — a nontrivial number of his marginal voters think that he will personally make them rich. But this also plays into a larger narrative that electing Republicans is good for business. But while electing Republicans is certainly good for many individual businesspeople and executives, as Catherine Rampell observes it’s not actually good for the economy as a whole : IN THE MIDST OF AN ENERGY CRISIS, the Trump administration is paying companies to not build more energy infrastructure. You read that correctly. The Trump administration is proudly shelling out almost $2 billion in exchange for agreements from developers to halt wind projects that were already in the works. These projects—wind farms off the coasts of New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, and California—would have generated enough electricity to power roughly 3-4 million households once they came online. What pot of taxpayer money this payoff comes from, and whether the payoffs are even legal , remain open questions. Even lawmakers don’t know the answers . But these companies should probably count themselves lucky. They got paid to shut down, after all. As an aside, paying people not to build clean energy projects is an excellent illustration of where the Republican Party is in 2026. It’s not even a question of their being a tradeoff between cleaner energy and costs — they’re willing to incur costs to make energy production dirtier because they just hate clean energy on principle. Dozens of other renewable energy projects around the country have not received the same—forgive me—windfall. Instead, they’ve been canceled, stalled, or quietly snuffed out, with zero compensation. The list includes a gargantuan solar project in Nevada and more than 150 wind developments around the country, which collectively could have powered around 15 million homes. 2 As a Heatmap headline declared: “Trump is getting away with murdering an American industry.” Renewables are hardly Trump’s only victim. Our president is basically an economic serial killer, whacking firms left and right. In addition to the intentional corporate executions—like the deliberate, gleeful “murders” of these wind projects—there are a great many Trump casualties that look more like manslaughter: companies the president is killing by accident, as with the recently departed Spirit Airlines (R.I.P.). The president simply doesn’t understand how his economic agenda of trade wars and hot wars might accidentally kill off already-weakened individual companies—or, in some cases, entire industries. Just over a year into his second term, the economic landscape is littered with victims mangled by Trump’s agenda. Corporate bankruptcies last year rose to their highest level in over a decade. So join me, friends, for a stroll through the ‘America First’ Corporate Graveyard. As she goes on to detail, whether it’s family farmers , manufacturers, of businesses dependent on cheaper fuel or cheaper imported goods and materials — all predominantly Trump constituencies and all getting completely screwed. And I don’t know what do about this any more than I know what to do about people who think that unilateral disarmament will save American democracy rather than crushing it. The post Bad for Business appeared first on Lawyers, Guns & Money .

The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. By JENNIFER McDERMOTT California is investigating one of the Trump administration’s deals to end an offshore wind project. Golden ...

The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. By JENNIFER McDERMOTT California is investigating one of the Trump administration’s deals to end an offshore wind project. Golden State Wind was a floating offshore wind project proposed off California’s central coast. The California Energy Commission said Monday it issued an administrative subpoena to Golden State Wind. The commission said it is seeking documents and information about the company’s recent agreement with the Department of Interior to accept a payout in exchange for voluntarily abandoning its offshore wind lease. “The Trump administration is recklessly spending billions of taxpayer dollars on backroom deals that would turn back the clock on innovation,” CEC Chair David Hochschild said in a statement. “Californians deserve immediate answers about the nature of this payout. Taxpayer dollars should be used to build a sustainable energy future, not to pay to make projects disappear.” The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said companies were sold a product that was only viable when propped up by massive taxpayer subsidies when they bid for these offshore wind leases in 2022, under former President Joe Biden. The Republican administration adopted this strategy after federal courts thwarted President Donald Trump’s efforts to stop offshore wind development through executive action. Three agreements have been announced. Under the first deal, made public in March, French company TotalEnergies is getting $1 billion essentially a refund of its leases for offshore wind projects off North Carolina and New York if it invests the money in fossil fuel projects instead. In the latest deals announced last week, the administration said Golden State Wind and Bluepoint Wind agreed to end their leases in exchange for reimbursements totaling nearly $900 million, provided they invest equally in fossil fuels. Both Golden State and Bluepoint are co-owned by Ocean Winds, a joint venture of EDP Renewables and French energy giant Engie. Bluepoint Wind was an offshore wind project in the early stages of development off the coasts of New Jersey and New York. When asked about the subpoena Monday, Ocean Winds said it does not comment on open or potential litigation. This investigation sets the stage for legal action from California to safeguard renewable energy, as well as the thousands of jobs and millions of dollars of investment the state was counting on, said Eddie Ahn, executive director of Brightline Defense, an environmental justice nonprofit working to advance offshore wind in California. A letter from California Attorney General Rob Bonta’s office to Golden State Wind says the state anticipates potential litigation involving the federal government and parties to lease buyouts impacting California’s energy needs and offshore wind programs. California has invested about $100 million to support offshore wind development in order to accelerate the state’s transition to clean energy and address climate change. Democrats in Congress are investigating, too. U.S. Reps. Jared Huffman of California, the top Democrat on the House Natural Resources Committee, and Jamie Raskin, the ranking Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee, are demanding information about TotalEnergies agreement.

May 10th, 2026 (Baseball News Source — Delivered by Newstex)

NewJersey Resources Corporation (NYSE:NJR -  Get Free Report) Director Jane Kenny sold 8,000 shares of the business's stock in a transaction that occurred on Friday, May 8th. The shares were sold at an average price of $56.10, for a total value of $448,800.00. Following the transaction, the director directly owned 21,998 shares in the company, valued at approximately $1,234,087.80. This represents a 26.67% decrease in their position. The transaction was disclosed in a legal filing with the SEC, which is available at  the SEC website.

NewJersey Resources Trading Up 0.1%

NJR traded up $0.04 during trading hours on Monday, reaching $55.59. 40,873 shares of the company's stock traded hands, compared to its average volume of 547,485. The firm has a 50-day moving average price of $55.13 and a 200 day moving average price of $50.33. NewJersey Resources Corporation has a 52-week low of $43.46 and a 52-week high of $57.85. The company has a quick ratio of 0.80, a current ratio of 0.94 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 1.24. The firm has a market capitalization of $5.61 billion, a PE ratio of 16.50 and a beta of 0.51.

NewJersey Resources (NYSE:NJR -  Get Free Report) last posted its quarterly earnings data on Monday, May 4th. The utilities provider reported $2.20 earnings per share (EPS) for the quarter, beating analysts' consensus estimates of $1.89 by $0.31. NewJersey Resources had a return on equity of 14.58% and a net margin of 15.67%.The company had revenue of $939.40 million during the quarter, compared to analysts' expectations of $849.95 million. During the same quarter in the previous year, the business earned $1.78 EPS. NewJersey Resources's revenue was up 2.9% compared to the same quarter last year. NewJersey Resources has set its FY 2026 guidance at 3.480-3.630 EPS. As a group, sell-side analysts predict that NewJersey Resources Corporation will post 3.5 EPS for the current fiscal year.

NewJersey Resources Announces Dividend

The company also recently announced a quarterly dividend, which will be paid on Wednesday, July 1st. Shareholders of record on Wednesday, June 10th will be issued a $0.475 dividend. This represents a $1.90 annualized dividend and a dividend yield of 3.4%. The ex-dividend date is Wednesday, June 10th. NewJersey Resources's dividend payout ratio is currently 56.38%.

Hedge Funds Weigh In On NewJersey Resources

Hedge funds have recently bought and sold shares of the business. Norges Bank purchased a new position in shares of NewJersey Resources in the fourth quarter valued at approximately $60,075,000. Millennium Management LLC raised its position in NewJersey Resources by 895.7% in the third quarter. Millennium Management LLC now owns 677,352 shares of the utilities provider's stock valued at $32,614,000 after purchasing an additional 609,323 shares during the period. Dimensional Fund Advisors LP raised its position in NewJersey Resources by 10.3% in the fourth quarter. Dimensional Fund Advisors LP now owns 3,934,165 shares of the utilities provider's stock valued at $181,446,000 after purchasing an additional 367,844 shares during the period. Qube Research & Technologies Ltd grew its stake in shares of NewJersey Resources by 77.1% in the second quarter. Qube Research & Technologies Ltd now owns 730,300 shares of the utilities provider's stock valued at $32,732,000 after buying an additional 317,932 shares in the last quarter. Finally, Hotchkis & Wiley Capital Management LLC acquired a new stake in shares of NewJersey Resources in the third quarter valued at $13,351,000. Hedge funds and other institutional investors own 70.98% of the company's stock.

Analyst Upgrades and Downgrades

A number of brokerages recently weighed in on NJR. Mizuho upped their price objective on NewJersey Resources from $54.00 to $61.00 and gave the stock an "outperform" rating in a research note on Tuesday, April 21st. Wall Street Zen raised NewJersey Resources from a "sell" rating to a "hold" rating in a research report on Saturday, February 7th. Raymond James Financial reaffirmed an "outperform" rating on shares of NewJersey Resources in a research report on Tuesday, February 3rd. Argus raised NewJersey Resources to a "strong-buy" rating in a research report on Thursday, January 22nd. Finally,  Weiss Ratings raised NewJersey Resources from a "hold (c+)" rating to a "buy (b-)" rating in a research report on Friday, February 13th. One research analyst has rated the stock with a Strong Buy rating and four have issued a Buy rating to the company's stock. According to MarketBeat.com, the stock currently has a consensus rating of "Buy" and a consensus price target of $54.80.

Check Out Our Latest Stock Analysis on NewJersey Resources

NewJersey Resources Company Profile

(Get Free Report)

New Jersey Resources Corporation is a publicly traded energy services holding company headquartered in Wall Township, New Jersey. The firm's primary focus is on the safe and reliable distribution of natural gas, along with complementary energy services and renewable energy investments. Its operations center on delivering cost-effective solutions to residential, commercial and industrial customers throughout the state.

The company's principal subsidiary, New Jersey Natural Gas, owns and operates an extensive pipeline network that spans northern, central and southern New Jersey.

urn:contentItem:6JJP-GB43-RS97-513J-00000-00Big Oil: Michigan antitrust lawsuit is purely political2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:00:47ZLexisNexisBig Oil: Michigan antitrust lawsuit is purely political

May 11th, 2026 (The Center Square: Michigan — Delivered by Newstex)

The oil industry is attacking Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel's attempt to use antitrust laws in a federal lawsuit alleging companies conspired to hold down competition from renewable-energy products.

Nessel sued companies like Exxon, Chevon and BP in January, alleging a #8216;cartel#8217; to maintain dominance in the transportation and primary energy markets that left consumers paying high prices. The companies responded earlier this month in a motion to dismiss that said her claims require more than speculation.

#8216;This is not an antitrust case,#8217; the motion says. #8216;It is an attempt by Michigan to use the antitrust laws to advance its current policy goals of promoting renewable energy over fossil fuels.

#8216;To state a claim, Michigan must plausibly plead standing, an actual agreement among Defendants that unreasonably restrains competition in a properly defined relevant market, and actionable conduct within the statute of limitations. The complaint pleas none of those things.#8217;

While the world waits for the U.S. Supreme Court to decide whether government officials can sue Big Oil for state-law claims like public nuisance and violations of consumer protection laws, Nessel embraced filing in federal court under the Sherman Antitrust Act, the Clayton Antitrust Act and the Michigan Antitrust Reform Act.

Her lawsuit alleged the companies abandoned renewable energy products, used patent manipulation and litigation to hinder competitors and suppressed information about the costs of fossil fuels and the viability of alternatives.

Her lawsuit said electric vehicles would be #8216;rolling off assembly lines in Flint#8217; had it not been for the oil industry. Solar, wind and other energies would power homes and businesses, she said. Fossil fuels would take a backseat if not for the conspiracy, the suit says.

#8216;Instead, Michigan consumers today remain locked in transportation and primary energy markets that have failed to evolve - not because clean alternatives are not viable, but because Defendants have suppressed the conditions for their otherwise-inevitable deployment and adoption,#8217; the complaint says.

The companies say these are points for a political debate - not an antitrust lawsuit - and Nessel can't show that if they invested more in renewable energy, innovation would have followed.

The U.S. Supreme Court is waiting on briefs in a lawsuit brought by Boulder and will decide if the Colorado Supreme Court was right to let it proceed. Hawaii's top court reached a similar ruling.

But Maryland's did not, affirming dismissals of cases in Baltimore and Annapolis. State judges in Pennsylvania, South Carolina, New York, Delaware and New Jersey also threw cases out as improper attempts to regulate emissions.

Alabama Attorney General Steve Marshall led a group of 26 states that said Boulder is trying to #8216;assert a power with no analogue in our Nation's history and no place in our federalism.#8217;

Republicans in Congress also filed a brief, complaining the Colorado Supreme Court's ruling created a patchwork of state and local regulations for a market that is supposed to be overseen by federal authorities.

And the Trump administration, which has issued an executive order forbidding any new cases and is battling Nessel in court, submitted its thoughts last year, saying Boulder is attempting to impose new rules that would go far beyond the borders of Colorado.

#8216;If, as the Colorado Supreme Court held, those theories are consistent with federal law, then every locality in the country could sue essentially anyone in the world for contributing to global climate change,#8217; that brief says
